channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 10 years in, the Try Guys expand their universe with a new streaming platform
item.description: Ten years ago, four Buzzfeed staffers took the site’s pivot to video as an opportunity to film themselves trying things they’d never done before. With a video of them trying on women’s underwear that went viral, the Try Guys were born.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Christensen, Fast Company

item.title: The AI Hype Bubble: A Reality Check on the Tech Sector's Future
item.description: Artificial intelligence investments reach an all-time high, but experts warn of a bubble forming in the tech sector. Will the hype settle down or lead to a market crash?
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Meta unveils Movie Gen, an AI model that can generate video with sound
item.description: Facebook owner Meta announced on Friday it had built a new AI model called Movie Gen that can create realistic-seeming video and audio clips in response to user prompts, claiming it can rival tools from leading media generation startups like OpenAI and ElevenLabs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, The Hill

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tener prisa por alcanzar el ROI de la IA puede salir caro
item.description: Muchas organizaciones han luchado por encontrar el retorno de la inversión (ROI) después de lanzar proyectos de inteligencia artificial (IA), pero existe el peligro de exigir demasiado, demasiado pronto, según la firma de investigación y asesoramiento de TI Forrester.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Things get nasty in lawsuit between WordPress.org and WP Engine
item.description: After years of nasty back and forth accusations between WordPress hosting provider WP Engine (WPE) and WordPress.org, the open source project that provides the software, on Wednesday WPE sued WordPress.org in federal court, accusing WordPress.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Amazon faces NLRB complaint over refusing to bargain with drivers’ union
item.description: Amazon.com has been accused by a U.S. labor board of illegally refusing to bargain with a union representing drivers employed by a contractor, the agency announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.94 improves file search
item.description: The September 2024 release of Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor, version 1.94, features improvements for finding files using the File Explorer. The upgrade also introduces the ability to run Python tests with coverage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California schools will be required to integrate AI into curriculum
item.description: New California law will require schools to incorporate AI literacy into math, science, and history-social science curricula.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Macasero, The Mercury News

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk’s friendship with Diddy examined after he posted about boy’s alleged abuse
item.description: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' mentee Aubrey O'Day and others called out Musk for trying to distance himself from allegations that the music mogul sexually abused multiple victims, including a 9-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: The US and Microsoft disrupt a Russian hacking group targeting American officials and nonprofits
item.description: U.S. authorities and Microsoft say hackers linked to Russian intelligence targeted dozens of former military and intelligence officials, journalists and civil society groups with a sophisticated campaign designed to steal information.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google CEO eyeing electricity from nuclear plants for its data centers
item.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai is considering using electricity from nuclear plants to power his company’s data centers, as the tech giant contends with the expansive energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Musk donated millions to GOP causes as early as 2022: Reports
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk donated millions of dollars to Republican causes as early as 2022, much earlier than previously known, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI on the mainframe? IBM may be onto something
item.description: CIOs have been moving workloads from legacy platforms to the cloud for more than a decade but the rush to AI may breathe new life into an old enterprise friend: the mainframe. At least IBM believes so.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How not to waste 34 hours a month in meetings
item.description: None of the participants will ever forget this meeting, that much is certain. The venue was the US Department of Defense, starring a new Secretary of State responsible for procurement, who was to lead her first meeting with suppliers and colleagues from her own company.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft cheat sheets: Dive into Windows and Office apps
item.description: Need to get up to speed on the latest features in Excel? Wrestling with an old version of Word? Finding your way around Windows 11 or looking to get more out of Windows 10? Computerworld’s cheat sheets are easy-to-use guides to help you navigate Microsoft’s core productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use extension methods in C#
item.description: In the C# programming language, extension methods enable you to extend the functionality of existing types without modifying them or deriving new types from them using inheritance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DoJ revising vulnerability disclosure framework to encourage AI red teaming
item.description: DoJ says its policy will encourage independent security and safety research, but will large AI companies follow suit in encouraging vulnerability disclosure? The post DoJ revising vulnerability disclosure framework to encourage AI red teaming first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Women of color running for Congress disproportionately targeted by offensive speech on X: Report
item.description: Congressional candidates who are women, specifically those of color, are more likely than other candidates to be targeted by offensive or hate speech on the social platform X, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge partially dismisses FTC's antitrust lawsuit against Amazon
item.description: A federal judge on Monday partially dismissed the Federal Trade Commission’s (FTC) antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. However, few details are currently available about U.S. District Judge John Chun’s decision, which was filed under seal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The worst programmer I know
item.description: If all code is legacy code, then we are all maintainers. I carry an image in my head of a vast sea of silent, diligent, dedicated coders who silently maintain much of the code that makes the world go round. I carry this image because I am one of those coders.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JRuby 10 due to arrive in early-2025
item.description: JRuby, which dates back to 2001 as a Ruby language implementation for the JVM, is set to arrive at version 10 shortly after the new year. JRuby 10 promises to be fully compatible with Ruby 3.4.0, a planned update to Ruby that brings changes for frozen string literals and class updates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany’s antitrust authority to look into complaint against Microsoft
item.description: German file hosting cloud service provider Nextcloud appeared to have won a major victory Monday, when the country’s antitrust authority announced it would be initiating what the company described as “the first steps towards an investigation into anti-competitive behavior by Microsoft, including the
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Large language models hallucinating non-existent developer packages could fuel supply chain attacks
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have a serious “package hallucination” problem that could lead to a wave of maliciously-coded packages in the supply chain, researchers have discovered in one of the largest and most in-depth ever studies to investigate the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel’s Altera spinout launches FPGA products, software
item.description: Altera – Intel’s standalone company focused on FPGA hardware – introduced an array of FPGA hardware, software, and services at its annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Device42 Unveils InsightsAI to Simplify IT Inventory Management with Generative AI
item.description: Device42's new feature, InsightsAI, utilizes generative AI to simplify IT inventory management and asset tracking, reducing the need for manual query construction and enhancing overall efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Network World
